notes: SHIP v1.14.271 - SHIP B: INSTRUMENT (RULING R1 view rail + ortho, R4 EXPLODE; owner: "camera open" + "r4 yes" + ".270 verified, ship b"). AUTHORITY: the CODE-QUEUE rack lock named "camera" explicitly; the owner lifted THAT TERM ONLY in chat 2026-07-16 (INTEGRATION-STATE S47). The SCENE stays locked and is untouched here: no materials, lights (the JOHN-LOCKED section-A rig), fog, tone mapping, tray geometry/internals, type colours, bezel strips, floor, reflection or boot change. "The rack is scenery; the camera is an instrument." ==== R1 - TWO CAMERAS, held for the life of the scene, switched BY REFERENCE (never constructed per switch), both resized by doResize so the inactive one is correct the instant it is switched in; teardown unchanged (cameras hold no GPU resources). ISO = the JOHN-LOCKED .255 perspective at FOV 28, still the default and landing view - nothing about ISO moves, and it deliberately takes NO view-lock so its idle auto-spin behaves exactly as today. FRONT/REAR = OrthographicCamera, because a perspective camera physically cannot draw a true elevation: the top cap fans open above the eye line and the bottom trays below it, and that splay is the projection, not the rack. Ortho makes every U equal height - the owner's acceptance sentence ("it's gotta be leveled, like you're looking straight at the rack") made testable. TOP = ~58deg high-3/4 (reads the top AND the face; a true plan view would show only the top beam). Frustum derives from the rack itself - the rack is centred on y=0 (yBase = -RH/2) so a symmetric frustum frames it exactly; ORTHO_MARGIN 1.08 = 8 percent headroom. VERIFIED NUMERICALLY against the real dims: RH 16.32 spans -8.16..+8.16, halfH 8.81 >= 8.16 (8.0 percent headroom), halfW 28.67 >= rack half-width 3.0, camera distance 23.4..72.3 inside near 0.3 / far 500. zoom is this build's DISTANCE multiplier (D = fitDist*zoom) so ortho takes its RECIPROCAL (1->1, 0.55->1.82, 1.7->0.59); the 0.55-1.7 clamp is untouched. Shortest-angular-path on theta so FRONT<->REAR never spins the long way. ==== .264 STANDS, UNTOUCHED: vertical DRAG still never writes tElev; setView() is the ONLY elevation writer and a preset button has no scroll collision, which is what .264 was actually about. NEW: a preset sets a _viewLock that suppresses the idle auto-spin - a FRONT that slowly rotates is no longer an elevation. ISO sets no lock (unchanged); any real orbit drag releases the lock and hands the camera back to the tech. ==== R4 - EXPLODE. Rail BUTTON, toggle, never a gesture: tap stays read-only (Cold Aisle - you tap to READ; the thing must not move under you), and the 11px tap-vs-drag threshold is untouched. Targets-only: setExplode writes grp.userData.tExplodeZ = RD*0.36 + ((i%3)-1)*0.3; the ONE existing frame loop lerps position.z at 0.07. Never a direct position write. The (i%3-1) stagger IS the whole visual - a uniform explode puts every tray face at one Z and reads as a solid slab you cannot count; the sawtooth gives each tray its own visible edge. Translation ONLY, no tilt (per ruling - it risks self-shadow surprises under the locked rig). SHEET RULE, deterministic: opening ANY sheet stows the explode - the device sheet calls setExplode(false) directly from inside the scene's own closure (no hook needed), and the phase sheet calls window._reh3dStow, a hook that exists only while a 3D scene is live and is NULLED in teardown so it can never call setExplode on a disposed context. Closing a sheet does NOT re-explode. ==== TWO SELF-CAUGHT HOISTING BUGS node --check CANNOT SEE (both mine, both would have shipped silently): [1] trayGroups was first declared with the orbit state, AFTER the slots.forEach that pushes to it - var hoists the binding but not the assignment, so the push would have hit undefined at runtime. [2] _railEl/_railBtns/_explodeBtn are ASSIGNED in the rail block but were re-declared "var x = null" 300 lines LATER, which WIPED the references after assignment: setView's is-on toggle would have silently no-opped and teardown would have got null, stranding the rail in the DOM forever. Both fixed by declaring above first use; an audit now asserts decl-line < first-assign-line for all five. ==== RULE 1: the rail is its OWN row, deliberately. Appending FRONT|ISO|TOP|REAR|EXPLODE to .reh-3d-toggle would put SEVEN buttons on one no-wrap flex row (~390px+) and push the viewport on a 390px phone - the exact defect the GB300 reference file ships. PROVEN: intrinsic content 329px (one row at 390); forced to 320px and 280px containers it WRAPS to 2 rows and NEVER overflows (flex-wrap makes horizontal overflow structurally impossible). ==== VERIFIED LIVE: rail renders FRONT/ISO/TOP/REAR/EXPLODE with ISO is-on at boot; stow hook present and idempotent; TEARDOWN removes the rail AND the CABLES chip AND nulls the stow hook, strip intact; ortho frustum math checked numerically against the real rack dims. ?legacy=1 proven clean LIVE: rackElevation_render3D called 0 times, no rail, no strip, _reh3dStow undefined, phdock_open does not throw (the typeof guard holds where no 3D scene exists), host #ops-content. ==== NOT VERIFIED HERE - DEVICE ONLY, and it is the owner's own Ship B checklist: everything behind the frame loop. rAF NEVER FIRES in a hidden automation tab (proven again this session), so the loop never ran: the ortho RENDER, the level FRONT/REAR read, TOP's framing, the explode MOTION and its stagger, shortest-path animation, the idle-lock, raycast/tap targets after the projection swap, shadow behaviour on extended trays, and 55fps. The camera math is verified numerically; the pixels are the owner's eye. DEVIATIONS REPORTED: [a] the spec assumed presets write a tZoom target - this build has NO tZoom (zoom is immediate, not lerped), so a preset snaps zoom to 1 for a deterministic framing rather than adding new lerp machinery. [b] the spec asked for "Cold Aisle sized" rail buttons in the ".reh-3d-seg styling family" - those conflict: the family is 22px tall (padding 4px / font 10px), below the 44px gloved floor. Shipped the family for visual consistency with FLAT|3D|CABLES directly above; resizing the family is a separate owner call, not a drive-by on chrome he has already verified. Gates: node --check 4/0; CSS 12 blocks balanced; CRLF uniform; three-stamp .270->.271. John iPhone gate: [6] FRONT: rack is LEVEL, no splay, U heights visually equal top to bottom. [7] REAR: same from behind. [8] ISO: identical to .270 - the locked look, unchanged. [9] TOP: usable overhead read; drag during TOP still never tilts. [10] each preset animates shortest-path; no long-way spins FRONT<->REAR. [11] EXPLODE out: stagger visible, internals readable; toggle home: EVERY tray returns fully (no stuck-out trays - the source file's close bug is the regression to hunt). [12] explode out -> open a sheet -> explode stows; close sheet -> still stowed (no auto-re-explode). [13] tap-to-inspect works in BOTH projections and while exploded. [14] orbit/pinch feel unchanged in ISO; page scroll still owns vertical. [15] watch for tray shadows clipping out of the section-A shadow frustum when extended (~+1.6 z) - if they do, that is a minimal shadow-camera widen next ship, not an aesthetic change.
